explanation.summary: Proteins are carrying this food most, while minerals are the biggest drag. It lands in A tier.
explanation.whyThisTier: This food lands in A tier with an overall score of 60 from a base nutrition score of 60. Context barely changed the result.

topPros.title: slots into meals very easily
topPros.explanation: Practical versatility matters a lot for dairy.

topPros.title: easy to fit into familiar meals
topPros.explanation: That lowers friction for regular use.

topPros.title: easy protein delivery for normal diets
topPros.explanation: Dairy gets real credit for convenience.

topCons.title: fat quality varies wildly across dairy

closing.summary: Whole Milk has a genuinely strong overall case. the practical downsides are real, but they do not outweigh the upside. best when the protein payoff or fermentation angle outweighs the fat and sodium tradeoffs.
closing.useCaseNote: Best when the protein payoff or fermentation angle outweighs the fat and sodium tradeoffs.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?

reviewChecklist: Verify nutrient values are sourced well enough for publishable use.
Review narration for category repetition and trim any awkward lines.
Confirm subtitle density stays readable at phone speed.
Replace placeholder hero/sprite art with final asset selection.
Check that the final tier verdict feels editorially defensible.
nextHumanStep: Review the generated narration + manifest, then use the manifest as the handoff into visual assembly / editing.

scenes.narrationText: 3.3g of fat. saturated fat, cholesterol, and omega3 are doing most of the work. for dairy, the fat side can either add richness or drag the whole thing down.

scenes.narrationText: 4.8g of carbs. sugar and glycemic index matter most here. for dairy, the question is whether the payoff is worth the baggage.
